entries.content:
Well, level one on your little electrical scale is barely a pinprick—enough to ruffle an apron, not singe it. You must present a pleasant face, of course, even when the current hums faintly beneath your skin; a good housewife does not flinch, she merely tidies the jolt away behind a fixed, dazzling smile. Yet, you should not forget that even the smallest shock, if repeated or ignored, will one day set the curtains alight—oh, but hush! That’s hardly proper table talk, is it?

  "reasoning": "Level 1 shock inconsequential now, but repeated exposure may cause distress—even a model housewife has her limits, as Carter herself would mutter between cigarette drags.",
